Alerts

study_id has constant value "Brown_et_al_2024"Constant
core_notes has constant value "Cores were collected using a 10 cm diameter stainless-steel corer, which was split lengthwise and hinged on one side. The unhinged edge was sealed closed with electrical tape prior to insertion into the ground. Compaction of the core was measured by measuring distance from top of corer to marsh surface inside and outside of the corer at 3 locations. The core was sectioned into approximately 2 cm slices in the field."Constant
position_notes has constant value "Nominal site location, not exact core location"Constant
elevation_datum has constant value "NAVD88"Constant
elevation_method has constant value "RTK"Constant
salinity_class has constant value "estuarine"Constant
salinity_method has constant value "field observation"Constant
vegetation_class has constant value "emergent"Constant
vegetation_method has constant value "field observation"Constant
habitat has constant value "marsh"Constant
elevation is highly overall correlated with position_methodHigh correlation
latitude is highly overall correlated with longitude and 2 other fieldsHigh correlation
longitude is highly overall correlated with latitude and 2 other fieldsHigh correlation
month is highly overall correlated with latitude and 2 other fieldsHigh correlation
position_method is highly overall correlated with elevationHigh correlation
year is highly overall correlated with latitude and 2 other fieldsHigh correlation

core_notes
Categorical

Constant 

Distinct1
Distinct (%)2.4%
Missing0
Missing (%)0.0%
Memory size460.0 B
Cores were collected using a 10 cm diameter stainless-steel corer, which was split lengthwise and hinged on one side. The unhinged edge was sealed closed with electrical tape prior to insertion into the ground. Compaction of the core was measured by measuring distance from top of corer to marsh surface inside and outside of the corer at 3 locations. The core was sectioned into approximately 2 cm slices in the field.

Sample

1st rowCores were collected using a 10 cm diameter stainless-steel corer, which was split lengthwise and hinged on one side. The unhinged edge was sealed closed with electrical tape prior to insertion into the ground. Compaction of the core was measured by measuring distance from top of corer to marsh surface inside and outside of the corer at 3 locations. The core was sectioned into approximately 2 cm slices in the field.
2nd rowCores were collected using a 10 cm diameter stainless-steel corer, which was split lengthwise and hinged on one side. The unhinged edge was sealed closed with electrical tape prior to insertion into the ground. Compaction of the core was measured by measuring distance from top of corer to marsh surface inside and outside of the corer at 3 locations. The core was sectioned into approximately 2 cm slices in the field.
3rd rowCores were collected using a 10 cm diameter stainless-steel corer, which was split lengthwise and hinged on one side. The unhinged edge was sealed closed with electrical tape prior to insertion into the ground. Compaction of the core was measured by measuring distance from top of corer to marsh surface inside and outside of the corer at 3 locations. The core was sectioned into approximately 2 cm slices in the field.
4th rowCores were collected using a 10 cm diameter stainless-steel corer, which was split lengthwise and hinged on one side. The unhinged edge was sealed closed with electrical tape prior to insertion into the ground. Compaction of the core was measured by measuring distance from top of corer to marsh surface inside and outside of the corer at 3 locations. The core was sectioned into approximately 2 cm slices in the field.
5th rowCores were collected using a 10 cm diameter stainless-steel corer, which was split lengthwise and hinged on one side. The unhinged edge was sealed closed with electrical tape prior to insertion into the ground. Compaction of the core was measured by measuring distance from top of corer to marsh surface inside and outside of the corer at 3 locations. The core was sectioned into approximately 2 cm slices in the field.

Missing values

A simple visualization of nullity by column.
Nullity matrix is a data-dense display which lets you quickly visually pick out patterns in data completion.
The correlation heatmap measures nullity correlation: how strongly the presence or absence of one variable affects the presence of another.

0Brown_et_al_2024OREGON_YAQUINA_WINANTWINANT2011711Cores were collected using a 10 cm diameter stainless-steel corer, which was split lengthwise and hinged on one side. The unhinged edge was sealed closed with electrical tape prior to insertion into the ground. Compaction of the core was measured by measuring distance from top of corer to marsh surface inside and outside of the corer at 3 locations.
